items.description: Prominent long irregular curved white abrasion running roughly horizontally from near the left inner border across the top of the central dark leather field toward the right side.
items.scholarly_note: Deep surface scratch exposing lighter leather layers; mechanical damage from handling, impact, or shelving.

items.description: Curved white scratch originating near the lower right inner border and extending leftward and slightly upward across the bottom of the central panel.
items.scholarly_note: Companion abrasion to upper scratch; similar post-production mechanical origin.

items.description: Heavily abraded and frayed left vertical edge with substantial loss of leather surface, exposing lighter substrate and fibrous structure along the spine or hinge.
items.scholarly_note: Heavy cumulative wear from repeated opening, handling, and shelving of the codex over centuries.

items.description: Dense network of fine light-colored hairline scratches and scuff marks distributed across the entire central dark leather surface.
items.scholarly_note: Accumulated micro-abrasions from dust, cloth contact, and general use; typical age-related surface degradation.

items.description: All corners rounded, crushed, and with material loss; bottom left and top left corners show the most pronounced deformation and wear.
items.scholarly_note: Characteristic wear pattern in bound volumes from vertical storage, pulling from shelves, and placement on surfaces.

items.description: Localized wear and partial loss of gold tooled lines along the outer border, especially bottom edge and vertical sides; small floral corner motifs also affected.
items.scholarly_note: The border features continuous wavy scroll/vine tooling with floral terminals; minor gilt loss on high points and edges.

items.description: Tall vertical gold-tooled bands of stacked scroll and acanthus motifs on left and right sides of central panel show flattening and loss of definition on raised elements.
items.scholarly_note: Central decorative program consists of symmetrical opposing vertical panels of dense baroque-style foliage tooling; wear indicates handling abrasion.

items.description: Small incised or scratched marks near top center of the dark panel, including faint intersecting lines or chevron-like mark.
items.scholarly_note: Possibly incidental or a faint later mark; no clear textual or intentional graphic character visible at this resolution.

items.description: Isolated small dark spots and localized darker discolorations, most visible in upper half and near borders.
items.scholarly_note: Possible ink flecks, wax, or environmental soiling; not consistent with liquid staining or extensive discoloration.

scratches: Long curved abrasion across upper central panel (001)
Curved scratch in lower central panel (002)
Dense fine hairline scratches and scuffs across central field (004)
Small incised marks upper central area (008)
damage_notes: Severe abrasion and fraying along left spine edge with major material loss
All four corners rounded, crushed, and with leather loss
General surface scuffing and multiple linear scratches throughout central panel
Wear and flattening of gold tooling on outer borders and central vertical ornaments
Overall leather darkening, scuffing, and surface loss consistent with age and heavy use
summary: Image shows the front cover of Codex Regius (GKS 2365 4to) gold-tooled dark brown leather binding, not an interior text folio. No marginalia, doodles, pen trials, or scribal corrections present. Survey records extensive mechanical damage: prominent curved scratches in upper and lower central panel, heavy left-edge/spine wear, corner crushing, widespread fine scuffing, and wear to the elaborate gold tooling (outer wavy borders and tall central vertical scrollwork panels). Modern ruler at top provides scale.
